feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: May 22, 2024, Edition
entry.content:
No, your eyes do not deceive you. SAP Community Voice -- our long-time email newsletter -- is making the transition to What's New.

As announced in the May 22 edition of the newsletter, we're doing two more editions in June, then moving SAP Community Voice to What's New permanently.

To help everyone prepare for this change, we're starting to publish SAP Community Voice content to What's New now. In fact, the items here come straight from the May 22 edition of SAP Community Voice.

If you subscribe to the email newsletter, expect two more -- one on June 5 and June 19 -- before we make the permanent switch to What's New. After that, please make sure you've subscribed to What's New so you don't miss our biweekly breakdown of community highlights, news, and more!

For now, please enjoy these entries from our latest -- albeit a little late for here -- newsletter.

 

Featured Content

 

Participate in Global Running Day with Other SAP Community Members! 

We teased this event in the previous newsletter, and now we're happy to announce that one of our SAP Champions is driving a grassroots effort for Global Running Day. Runners will gather on June 5 to run the Tan in Melbourne, Australia, but you can participate virtually from June 1 to 9! 

Read the blog post

 

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q2 2024 Quarterly Release (QRC) 

The headline says it all: This blog post highlights the latest features in the Q2 2024 release of SAP Analytics Cloud. The post includes detailed information, as well as a video demonstrating what's new in this quarterly release. Our product experts cover everything! 

Read the blog post

 

First Steps for SAP Cloud ALM Deployment Scenario for ABAP Systems 

You'll need to set aside some time for this one, but once you read this thorough post, you'll be able to create a test landscape to practice with the SAP Cloud ALM deployment scenario without creating interferences with the real landscapes for the transport management system. 

Read the blog post

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration with SAP Datasphere

This post explains how to create a connection between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Datasphere, exploring integration strategies and real-world applications to achieve operational excellence, drive innovation and efficiency, and improve decision-making. 

Read the blog post

 

Community News

 

Getting the Most from SAP Community 

Per the introduction, What's New is the place to go for the latest announcements about SAP Community -- and it's also becoming the home for the SAP Community Voice newsletter. You'll want to subscribe here to stay up to date -- and also read our help section, watch our how-to videos, and take the Tour the SAP Community 2024 tutorial. 

 

Meet Your Peers Online and In Person at SAP Community Events! 

Global Running Day, a combination of in-person and virtual activities, isn't the only upcoming event scheduled by and for our members! Visit our events section to learn about SAP CodeJam, SAP Inside Track, and SAP Stammtisch meetups, as well as other online and in-person events.

 

Show Off Your Support for SAP Community Offline 

When you log out from SAP Community, do you still want the world to see how much your community membership means to you? Then you'll want to pick up something from our online store. We offer a variety of shirts, hoodies, socks, and more -- all proudly displaying the SAP Community name, so you can look stylish and smart at the same time.

entry.title: A Importância do Engajamento e Participação nos SAP Inside Track
entry.content:
A participação das Comunidades SAP:
O ecossistema SAP é vasto, com milhões de usuários, desenvolvedores, consultores e especialistas em todo o mundo. O engajamento dentro dessa comunidade vai além de simplesmente utilizar os produtos SAP; trata-se de compartilhar experiências, solucionar desafios, propor inovações e fortalecer os laços profissionais e pessoais entre os membros.

A participação ativa em fóruns online, grupos de discussão, redes sociais e eventos presenciais é fundamental para aproveitar ao máximo o potencial da comunidade SAP. Essa interação contínua não só amplia o conhecimento individual, mas também contribui para o avanço coletivo, enriquecendo a base de conhecimento disponível para todos.

Eventos Tecnológicos e o SAP Inside Track:
Entre os diversos eventos dedicados à tecnologia SAP, o SAP Inside Track destaca-se como uma iniciativa significativa. Organizados por membros da comunidade, esses eventos são espaços informais e colaborativos onde profissionais SAP se reúnem para compartilhar conhecimento, discutir tendências, apresentar projetos e estabelecer conexões pessoais e profissionais.

O SAP Inside Track oferece uma plataforma única para o aprendizado contínuo e o networking dentro da comunidade SAP. Com uma agenda diversificada que abrange desde sessões técnicas avançadas até workshops práticos, esses eventos atendem às necessidades de uma ampla gama de profissionais, desde iniciantes até especialistas.

Importância da Organização e Participação:
Tanto a organização quanto a participação nos eventos SAP, como o Inside Track, desempenham papéis cruciais no fortalecimento da comunidade e no avanço do conhecimento. Os organizadores dedicam tempo e esforço para criar programas relevantes, atrair palestrantes qualificados e garantir uma experiência enriquecedora para os participantes.

Por outro lado, a participação ativa dos membros é essencial para o sucesso desses eventos. Ao compartilhar suas experiências, insights e desafios, os participantes contribuem para a diversidade e a riqueza das discussões, promovendo um ambiente de aprendizado colaborativo e inclusivo.

Conclusão:
O engajamento das comunidades SAP e a participação em eventos tecnológicos, como o SAP Inside Track, são pilares essenciais para o desenvolvimento profissional e o progresso do ecossistema SAP. Ao interagir, colaborar e compartilhar conhecimento, os membros dessa comunidade fortalecem os laços profissionais, impulsionam a inovação e garantem o sucesso contínuo das soluções SAP no mundo empresarial em constante evolução. Portanto, é fundamental reconhecer e valorizar o papel dessas comunidades e eventos na promoção do aprendizado contínuo e no crescimento profissional de todos os envolvidos.

Manuel Robalinho

@MRobalinho

SAP Community

entry.title: Re-imagining Innovation - Begin of SIT – ICOE AI hackathon – 2024
entry.content:
“Dream, Code, Achieve”

The recent AI Hackathon event was a remarkable success, marking a significant milestone in our ongoing efforts to foster technological innovation and collaboration. The event brought together talented students, industry experts, and academic leaders, creating a vibrant atmosphere of learning, creativity, and future-forward thinking.

Event & Certification Partners:

  • International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI, and Futuristic Technologies
  • SAP Insider Track Community Kolkata
  • Alien Brains Educations
  • Westernacher Consulting
  • Galelio AG 

Who participated?

All the engineering college students in West Bengal, All the Students from Colleges and Universities in Science and Commerce and relevant departments were eligible for the AI hackathon event. This hackathon was conducted in 2 Rounds.

Hackathon Timeline

11th April 2024: Round 1 (240 marks MCQ-based online exam)

12th April 2024: Round 1 Result Declaration (Top 25 teams advance to Round 2)

12th - 22nd April 2024: Round 2 (10-day Coding problem statement)

22nd April 2024: Final Submission Date of Round 2

25th April 2024: Announcement of Top 10 Teams

27th April 2024: SIT Community Event and Felicitation of Top 10 Teams

Core Team and Mentors

We are proud to have a distinguished panel of mentors and core team members guiding this event:

Prof. Amlan Chakrabarti: COO, International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI & Futuristic Technologies

Dr. Anindita Ganguly: Member, International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI & Futuristic Technologies

Dr. Saptarsi Goswami: Member, International Center of Excellence for Data Science, AI & Futuristic Technologies

Sabyasachi Mukhopadhyay: Research Scholar (IIT KGP), Faculty, Google Developer Expert (ML)

Praveen Kumar Bahety: Founder, MD, Alien Brains, Sociana

Srini Gottimukkula: SIT VIP Panelist

Dipankar Saha: SAP Author & Mentor

 

Problem Statement

Theme:   Application of AI in Education

Topic 1:  AI for automated Essay Grading

  • Expectation is to find public datasets, with essays from students and grades by teachers.  
  • Build a model which takes the essay as an input assigns a score ( Free to use different open source models and fine tune)
  • More credit will be given which will explain the reason behind the score

Ref:

kaggle.com/competitions/asap-aes 

github.com/sankalpjain99/Automatic-Essay-Scoring 

frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/feduc.2020.572367/full 

 

Topic 2:  Bengali to English Translation for Science Subjects

  • Expectation is to find, build datasets with English to Bengali Translation
  • Build or leverage models (Free to explore openAI,  Bhasini etc.) for translation

Ref:

https://ieee-dataport.org/open-access/supara-benchmark-benchmark-dataset-english-bangla-machine-translation 

https://huggingface.co/saiful9379/Bangla-to-English-Translation 

 

Topic 3:  Student Performance Analysis

  • Expectation is to find public datasets with student performance
  • Correlate performance in different disciplines
  • Correlate student background with performance

Ref:

archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/320/student+performance 

https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038947/Learning-Poverty-Global-Database--Historical-data-and-sub-components 

Deliverable:

  • Working prototype in any environment like Google-colab
  • Presentation Explaining the same
  • Writeup should include future implementation plan with roadmap

A slot of 20 minutes will be given between 23rd to 25th April for the presentation of the same

30 marks on Demonstration 10 marks on presentation and 10 marks on Writeup

 

The Top 10

 

Sayandip Bhattacharyya

Generative Solutions

Crypto Coders

Cuber Savants

Zeus

Techie

Algorithm Architects

Web Homie

CamCoderr

Tech Strivers

Continued Support for Top Teams

In alignment with the advice of Dr. Anindita Ganguly, Alien Brains has decided to provide free guidance sessions to the Top 10 teams. This initiative aims to help these teams enhance their current projects significantly. Furthermore, once the ICOE campus is complete, these top-performing teams may receive an invitation for a special guidance session with ICOE professors, providing them with invaluable insights and mentorship.

Celebrating Success and Collaboration

Thanks to everyone involved for their opportunity and support throughout this journey. The event was a resounding success, with all participating students expressing their happiness and enthusiasm. We are pleased to announce that they have received their well-deserved certificates, symbolizing their hard work and dedication. It was a great experience for me to mentor and have experienced real time hackathon and looking forward to many more in Collab with @DataTherapist and other AI enthusiastic in the community.

entry.title: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Earlier this month I had the opportunity to interview Eric van Rossum, SAP’s Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP. You can watch the video of our discussion and read the highlights below, where you’ll also find related learning materials. Enjoy!

Eric van Rossum is the Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP at SAP, overseeing product marketing for the company's cloud ERP portfolio. He serves as the strategic bridge between product development and customer acquisition, ensuring product messaging and positioning are attuned to customer needs, and facilitating the successful launch and adoption of products in the market. Eric has been with SAP for 24 years and brings a wealth of experience across various functions including consulting, sales, corporate strategy, and support.

To help me summarize the content of the interview, I've used SAP Generative AI Hub :-). Check here what it ays are the highlights of our conversation: 

  • SAP has two cloud ERP offerings: RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP is targeted toward SAP’s installed-base customers, providing them a path to cloud ERP at their pace.
  • GROW with SAP is targeted toward net new customers to SAP, and installed base customers who want to take a greenfield approach to cloud ERP.
  • SAP is focusing on making ERP an enabler for business model changes, driving sustainability, and improving efficiency, as these are the key customer needs currently in the market.
  • With RISE and GROW, customers should be able to update their software regularly, making them innovation-ready and efficient, especially with the increasing prevalence of AI.
  • Eric believes the shift to the cloud also requires a paradigm shift within companies, citing an example of a customer who moved towards a standardized system and adopted SAP's out-of-the-box solutions.
  • One of the significant hurdles for customers adopting cloud ERP is the organizational readiness to standardize their processes, which requires a significant cultural shift.
  • Moving ERP to the cloud is seen as risky by some companies due to the critical role ERP systems play in company operations. They need strategies to transition without disrupting their business.
  • Eric is interested in a continued dialogue with the SAP Community, to hear from end users and improve SAP’s offerings to meet their needs better.

If you’re going to Sapphire in Orlando or Barcelona, you can hear more from Eric in these sessions:

SAP Sapphire Orlando (June 3-5)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP169)

  • Supercharge Your Business and Get Innovation Ready with RISE with SAP (Session code: ERP168)

  • The New ERP Mindset (Session code: TRE321)

SAP Sapphire Barcelona (June 11-13)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP146)

If you’re planning to join Sapphire virtual, you can hear more in these sessions:

  • ERP126 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Discover planned innovations

  • ERP127 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what is next

  • ERP200v | Accelerate business growth with S/4AHANA Cloud Public Edition

And to learn more about the topics discussed in this interview, the following learning journeys are especially recommended:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP learning journey as a starting point to start learning about SAP Cloud ERP, how to distinguish SAP’s cloud ERP solutions and transformation offerings (GROW & RISE), establish a cloud mindset (fit-to-standard, SAP best practices, integration and extensibility, clean core), and get insights into business AI and sustainability fundamentals
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learning journey teaching fundamental implementation knowledge for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leading to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation consultant certification complemented by quarterly live sessions, stay certified content and assessments focusing on AI capabilities and intelligent ERP innovations
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey teaching implementation fundamentals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition leading to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation consultant certification, complemented by stay certified content and assessments focusing on how to Manage a Clean Core

Those fundamentals learning journeys are complemented by various line of business specific learning offerings and certifications focusing on business processes, best practices content, and configuration/integration/extensibility options

entry.title: Logistics Simplified: Resources, Insights, Workshops, and More for Smooth Adoption
entry.content:
Introducing you to the SAP Digital Logistics Adoption Campaigns!

Our mission is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to fully leverage the capabilities of Logistics solutions. This program is designed to help you understand the unique value these solutions bring to your business, guiding you through every step of the adoption journey. You’ll find comprehensive resources, expert insights, customer experience sharing, and practical tips to accelerate implementation, ensuring a smooth Go-Live, and maximizing the benefits for your business!

Our current campaign focus is on S/4HANA Transportation Management and S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management. Join us on this journey through just a few simple steps!

Step 1: Access the Solution Value Card

A value card is a one-pager with key information on the solution, such as its value proposition, key capabilities, and quick recourses. It also indicates what to expect from the Adoption campaign. 

  • S/4HANA Transportation Management
  • S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

Step 2: Participate in Expert Guided Adoption Series

To further support your journey, we have organized workshops that are crafted to provide in-depth training, featuring interactive sessions, real-world scenarios, and expert-led discussions. Whether you're just getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, these sessions will equip you with actionable strategies and best practices to drive successful adoption and consumption. Join us to enhance your skills, connect with peers, and challenge us with your unique questions so we can understand your needs better!

S/4HANA Transportation Management

  • Getting Started with S/4HANA Transportation Management: PDF | Recording
  • June 25, 2024: Baseline Activation and Setups
  • July 18, 2024: Optimizer & Package Building
  • August 20, 2024: Insights & Analytics

S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

  • Overview & Value Realization: PDF | Recording
  • Feature Implementation Best Practices: PDF | Recording
  • June 13, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Consumer Products
  • June 27, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Automotive
  • July 11, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Retail
  • August 8, 2024: Business AI Adoption in Logistics

We hope these sessions enhance your understanding and ability to leverage the solution, and we hope to see you in one of our upcoming workshops! If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to email us.

entry.title: Empower Your Enterprise: SAP BRIM Explained
entry.content:
During the SAP Inside Track Kolkata 2024 event, the renowned speaker Swapna Borra captivated the audience with an extraordinary live session on SAP BRIM – Entitlement Management. Her presentation was nothing short of phenomenal, delivering profound insights and showcasing the robust capabilities and advantages of SAP BRIM in entitlement management. The session left an indelible mark on the attendees, underscoring the transformative potential of this technology.

Optimizing Entitlement Management with SAP BRIM

SAP BRIM (Billing and Revenue Innovation Management), previously known as SAP Hybris Billing, is a comprehensive billing solution designed for industries with high-volume consumption. It simplifies the intricate billing and invoicing processes, supporting flexible consumption models. SAP BRIM facilitates a range of flexible consumption models. As a modular solution, it is crafted to enhance the business lifecycle processes, including design, sales, delivery, and billing. This versatile solution is utilized across various sectors, including telecommunications, financial services, utilities, media, logistics, and public transport.

SAP BRIM Components

The solution comprises four core components:

  • SAP BRIM, subscription order management (SOM)
  • SAP Convergent Charging (CC)
  • SAP Convergent Invoicing (CI)
  • SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA)

Collectively, these components assist in overseeing business model design, executing order management, monitoring usage metering and transaction pricing, handling billing and invoice processing, managing receivables and payables, royalties, and commissions, and offering support for financial customer care.

Some of the Use Cases across Industries:

Features of SAP BRIM:

Difference between the version of SAP BRIM Solution Architecture:

The components of the SAP BRIM solution are available both as integrated areas within SAP S/4HANA and as standalone modules that seamlessly interface with SAP S/4HANA.

The diagram below demonstrates how each core component of SAP BRIM fits within the end-to-end solution landscape. SAP BRIM and subscription order management, along with SAP Convergent Charging, function as standalone applications and are deployed in separate instances, indicated by (1) for SAP BRIM and subscription order management, and (2) for SAP Convergent Charging. Conversely, SAP Convergent Invoicing and FI-CA are integral parts of the SAP S/4HANA solution, included by default with SAP S/4HANA. These are represented in the diagram with (3), corresponding to SAP S/4HANA, FI-CA, and SAP Convergent Invoicing.

Fig. SAP BRIM Older Version – 2018

In the new architecture of SAP S/4HANA 1909, subscription order management has been integrated into the SAP S/4HANA solution, as shown below. This integration greatly simplifies the implementation and integration processes for companies adopting the SAP BRIM solution.

 

Fig. SAP BRIM New version

Subscription Order Management (SOM)

In the latest version of S4/HANA, Subscription Order Management (SOM) has become a crucial component of the comprehensive BRIM solution. This enhancement is an additional function built upon SAP CRM. Previously, SAP CRM served as the primary interface for businesses to interact with customers for service and product selection.

SAP SOM and CRM serve as the starting point for SAP BRIM, managing the entire product catalog, orders, distribution creation, and contract lifecycle management. It is implemented with Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) to allow for flexible product configuration. Once contracts and orders are established in SOM, they are replicated to SAP FI-CA and SAP Convergent Charging for further processes, such as invoicing, billing, charging, and rating.

Fig. Subscription Order Management

SAP BRIM, with its subscription order management module, enables companies to efficiently manage their customers through a range of specialized features designed to flexibly meet customer needs. These features include:

  • Product catalog and offer management.
  • Order capture
  • Order distribution
  • Contract management
  • Customer care

 

     

 

Convergent Charging (CC)

SAP Convergent Charging is engineered to provide pricing flexibility for customer charging. Within SAP Convergent Charging, a charge refers to the price derived from a rating and charging event, which is billed to the customer (or subscriber). Essentially, SAP Convergent Charging acts as a calculation engine, determining various types of charges, including recurring, one-time, and usage-based fees.

Fig. Convergent Charging

The figure above depicts the integration of SAP Convergent Charging within the SAP BRIM solution. SAP Convergent Charging consists of two primary components: charging and rating. Charging is the process by which SAP Convergent Charging identifies the specific customer account to be billed. Conversely, rating involves the calculation method that determines the total amount to be charged in the billing document.

Convergent Invoicing (CI)

Convergent Invoicing (CI) offers a versatile solution for automating billing and invoicing processes. It begins by receiving inputs (such as complete bills, recurring charges, or rated events) from one or multiple billing or rating systems. These inputs are stored based on their status and consolidated into a single invoice for consumers, along with an individual outstanding receivable.

Fig. Convergent Invoicing

SAP CI seamlessly integrates multiple billing sources into a unified, streamlined invoice from various origins. Once the rated and charged items enter SAP CI, they are stored as billable products, and the processes of billable item aggregation, invoice creation, and billing are carried out efficiently.

Contract Account Receivable and Payable (FI-CA)

This is the final fundamental component of SAP BRIM handles financial documents, credits and collections, dispute management, and payment processing. SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA) serves as the financial backend, providing sub-ledger accounting tailored to meet the needs of industries that engage with numerous customers and transactions. FI-CA offers a comprehensive suite of features, including tax calculations and compliance with country-specific procedures such as partner settlement, collection management, payment processing, and various accounting principles.

Fig. Contract Accounting

SAP Entitlement Management

SAP Entitlement Management simplifies and automates entitlement processes for companies, efficiently managing the entire entitlement lifecycle (i.e., overseeing what customers are entitled to). This solution ensures clear visibility for both customers and sales representatives, providing alerts, reminders, and other notifications. As a result, customer satisfaction is enhanced, and opportunities for upselling and cross-selling are significantly increased.

Fig. Entitlement Management Architecture

Fig. Entitlement Management – Use Cases

Conclusion:

SAP BRIM is a robust solution designed for managing subscriptions and other recurring revenue streams. At a high level, the process begins with order creation in SAP BRIM's subscription order management. Calculations are then performed in SAP Convergent Charging based on usage, resulting in the final charge amounts. Bills and invoices are generated and sent to customers via SAP Convergent Invoicing. Finally, in contract accounting, financial documents are posted, and payments are received or made. Contract accounting also facilitates dispute management, credit management, and collections.

 

@DataTherapist 
@Swapnaborra

entry.title: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 – Business AI Edition
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the speaker registration for SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 – Business AI Edition, happening on July 6th, 2024! This year, our focus is on the transformative power of SAP Business AI, and we are looking for passionate speakers to share their expertise and insights across various tracks. If you have experience in building AI applications, creating innovative Business AI solutions, or working with Embedded AI in SAP products, we invite you to contribute to our event.

Event Details:

Date: July 6th, 2024
Time: 8 AM - 5 PM IST
Venue: SAP Labs India, 138, SAP Labs Rd, EPIP Zone, Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560066
 
Find more about the event in the announcement blog post: SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 - Business AI Edition - Announcement 

Speaker Tracks for your proposal:

Track 2: Building AI Applications on SAP BTP
Dive into the technicalities of building AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Demonstrate how developers and architects can leverage SAP BTP to create robust AI solutions that drive business value.

Track 3: Business AI Solutions from Ecosystem - Partners & Customers
Showcase successful AI solutions developed by SAP partners and customers. Share case studies, implementation experiences, and collaborative projects that highlight the innovative use of Business AI within the SAP ecosystem.

Track 4: Embedded AI across Solutions - SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors & Other SAP Products
Explore the integration of Embedded AI in various SAP products like SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, CX, and more. Discuss how these intelligent features are transforming business processes and enhancing user experiences.

Why Speak at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 - Business AI Edition?

  • Showcase Your Expertise: Share your knowledge with a passionate and engaged audience of SAP professionals.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with fellow experts, partners, and SAP enthusiasts.
  • Gain Recognition: Enhance your professional profile and gain recognition within the SAP community

How to Register:

Ready to share your insights at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 – Business AI Edition? Register now to become a speaker and contribute to one of the most exciting SAP community events of the year.

Speaker Registration Link: https://forms.gle/DbxTfpqHSf8KWW6z5

Next Steps: Once you submit your proposal,
Step 1: Our expert team will go through your proposal and if it is shortlisted, we will contact your for more details.
Step 2: This will again be discussed internally with the experts and once shortlisted, we will inform you about the next steps – like presentation format, social media advertisement etc.,

We look forward to your valuable contributions and to seeing you at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024!

Best Regards,
Mahesh

entry.title: SAP上海客户成功日-6.19号SAP Labs China
entry.content: 欢迎SAP客户和伙伴注册

entry.title: A Culinary Journey Through New York City
entry.content:
New York City is a paradise for food lovers, offering an unparalleled diversity of culinary experiences. Whether you're a local or a visitor, there are some iconic eats you simply cannot miss. Here's a curated guide to some of the best food spots in NYC, from high-end delis to budget-friendly food trucks.

Katz's Delicatessen

You can't leave NYC without trying Katz's Delicatessen. Famous for its pastrami sandwiches, this place serves up what many consider the best pastrami sandwich in history. It's a bit pricey at $30, but the portion is generous, and the flavor is unforgettable. Check it out on Google Maps.

Shake Shack and Best Burger

When it comes to unique and high-quality fast food, Shake Shack is ten steps above the rest. Known for its delicious burgers, shakes, and fries, Shake Shack offers a gourmet fast food experience that you won't forget. Find it here.

but although I haven't tried it yet, reviews and word of mouth suggest this place has the best burger in NYC:

  • Highly recommended burger spot

Bagels and Lox

For a classic NYC breakfast, head to the top bagel shops. While you can splurge on bagels with lox, it's worth every penny. These bagels are ubiquitous in the city, but some shops stand out for their exceptional quality. Here's a great place to start.

Chinatown's Culinary Gems

Chinatown offers some of the best and most affordable dining experiences in NYC. Two top restaurants to try for my perspective are:

  • First restaurant
  • Second restaurant

If you're looking for a budget-friendly option, this place is excellent:

  • Budget-friendly spot

For those are even shorter with budget, this is a great choice:

  • Very affordable option

Cheesecake in NYC

Heading north, you'll find some of the best cheesecakes in the city. The top spot for cheesecake is here, with a close second being this location.

Best Food Stall

For an iconic NYC street food experience, there's a food stall that's considered the best in the city. Despite the 30-40 minute wait, it's worth it, and for just $10, you'll leave feeling super full. Check it out.
I also made a blog about it here in the SAP Community.

Affordable Pizza

NYC is known for its dollar slices, but for the best cheap pizza, this spot stands out:

  • Best cheap pizza

I love art and this slice is perfect before or after the MoMa. If you are passionate of TV series round the corner there is the reference bar from How I Met Your Mother.


Financial District Food Trucks

The Financial District is home to some great food trucks. Usually at lunch a lot of people used to eat from a food truck. I worked for a while here and I often ate at an:

  • Indian food truck 

And my favorite is an excellent falafel truck nearby that's worth seeking out but it's not on the maps. It is in the Zuccotti Park check it out.

Embark on this culinary adventure and experience the diverse flavors that make New York City a food lover's dream. Enjoy!

entry.title: BTP博客索引
entry.content:
BTP

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)的架构理解 
  • BTP Acceleration加速服务介绍 
  • 如何免费申请BTP试用环境
  • BTP常见登陆问题 - 视频讲解 

Integration Suite (CPI) 集成套件

  • SAP Integration Suite集成套件2小时中文概览回放
  • SAP Integration Suite集成套件问答FAQ 
  • SAP S/4HANA Private连接SAP Integration Suite端到端教学演示 
  • SAP集成套件（CPI）证书更新手册 
  • SAP Integration Suite集成套件自助开通手册 
  • SAP Event Mesh 在SAP业务技术平台中国（上海）区域的入门级动手教程 - SAP Community

Application Development 应用开发

  • 基于 SAP 云平台的云应用授权管理 (上) 
  • 基于 SAP 云平台的云应用授权管理 (下) 

Build

  • 为您揭开 SAP Build 的神秘面纱 

Build Process Automation 流程自动化

  • SAP Build 流程自动化新用户入门指南 - SAP Community
  • SAP Build流程自动化中文合集 
  • 如何利用SAP Build Process Automation构建一个工作坊申请 

Build Apps 无代码开发

  • 如何开始SAP BTP无代码服务 - 构建一个端到端的BP展示页面 

Build Work Zone 门户网站

  • UI Integration Card to S/4 on SAP Build Work Zone Standard Version
  • 使用Business Application Studio开发SAP Build Work Zone插件

Identity Authentication Service 身份认证服务

  • 使用IAS 安全策略做权限管理
  • SAP 身份认证服务中文讲解视频 

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) 分析云

  • SAP Analytics cloud SAML SSO with BTP Cloud Identity services - Identity Authentication End to End From SAP Analytics cloud to the Live BW system
  • SAP分析云中文合集 

Document Management Service (DMS) 文档管理服务

  • 如何开始SAP BTP文档管理服务Document Management Service (DMS) 

Custom Domain 自定义域名

  • SAP BTP上实现自定义域名配置 - SAP Community

entry.title: Understand and Explore your SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition with our new E-Learning
entry.content:
Hello SAP Signavio Community, 

Do you need help reading and understanding your Process Insights Discovery Edition Report?  Not sure where to start?  If so, this E-Learning is for you!

In this E-Learning you will learn how to: 

  • Leverage SAP Signavio Process Insights for your organization
  • Identify process optimization potential for your company
  • Obtain recommendations to transform your processes

The SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition provides tailor-made recommendations, based on data extracted from your SAP ERP  (or SAP S/4 HANA) System, to help you:

  • Improve business processes
  • Start the journey to SAP S/4 HANA
  • Optimize live SAP S/4 HANA systems

“Understanding and Exploring your SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition” E-Learning

Requirement: Registration for the SAP Learning Hub is required in order to utilize e-Learning sessions.  If you are not currently registered, please do so here: SAP Enterprise Support Academy

For additional information please refer to the following Blog Posts:

  • Request your own SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition for free!
  • How the SAP Signavio Process Insights, discover edition can help in your role.

Recommendation: If you are not yet signed up for Value Maps, please join via this link.

You will discover new opportunities to understand and analyze the bottlenecks in business processes, and improve the identified challenges. The Business Process Transformation value map helps you to innovate your business processes in an ever-changing world.

Please don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation" topic).

entry.title: Introducing Business Builders: Free SAP gamified learning with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
[Check the official press release here Business Builders Prepares Future Leaders | SAP Sapphire | SAP News Center]

We are thrilled to introduce Business Builders, a gaming platform developed by HEC Montréal in close collaboration with us SAP 🎮 for educators around the world.

Business Builders offers gamified learning experiences that equip students with crucial data storytelling and decision-making skills needed for success in the modern business landscape while developing in-demand SAP Analytics Cloud skills.

With initial scenarios in key areas such as sustainable business practices, supply chain resilience, and international business expansion, Business Builders offers a comprehensive learning experience  to help students bring out their best in the competitive global job market. 🌍

And the best: This new educational offering is free to all educators and their students globally.

𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐲 𝐭𝐨 𝐛𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐭𝐨 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐥𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐦? 

Professors, lecturers and teachers can apply to register for a Business Builders account for free ➡ www.businessbuilders.games. Preregister for training starting June 25. Let’s make learning fun ✨ and impactful together! 

👍 Give us a kudos if you like these news! 👍

Your SAP University Alliances Team

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: June 5, 2024, Edition
entry.content:
As announced in the last two editions of SAP Community Voice, and in this What's New post, we are phasing out our newsletter.

We'll send out one final edition, scheduled for June 19. After that, SAP Community Voice will be available exclusively here, in this What's New section of the SAP Community site. In fact, we've already started to add SAP Community Voice content to What's New (but, since you're reading this post, you probably already guessed that).

If you enjoy SAP Community Voice content, then please subscribe to What's New now. It's easy. Just go to the What's New landing page, click the "Options" menu in the upper right, and select Subscribe.

By subscribing to What's New (and setting your notification settings to your liking), you'll still be able to receive the community news, highlights, and other content you've come to expect from SAP Community Voice.

We'll be back in approximately two weeks with a new SAP Community Voice post. For now, enjoy this sampling of popular SAP Community content.

 

Featured Content

 

Changes: SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories and Analytic Applications 

This blog post explains the decision behind the deprecation of the classic experience for this product, the benefits of the optimized experience, and the key timelines for the transition. By reading, you can prepare for the change and make the most of the optimized experience! 

Read the blog post

 

Don't Miss Your Chance to Join Global Running Day (Through June 9) 

June 5 is Global Running Day. If you couldn't participate on the actual date (either virtually or in person with other members at Tan in Melbourne, Australia), we have good news for you! You still have until June 9 to join the fun. Not a runner? Walkers are welcome, too! 

Read the blog post

 

Blog Series: SAP Datasphere Multilingual Support - Translation Dashboard 

The second in a series on multilingual capabilities in SAP Datasphere, this post provides guidance on how to use the new dashboard for translating metadata in SAP Datasphere that SAP Analytics Cloud can leverage to display the stories or dashboards in several language. 

Read the blog post

 

Extracting Data Sources from SAP S/4HANA ODP to SAP Datasphere

A data source enabled for operational data provisioning (ODP) allows the transfer of data between systems efficiently, and this blog post explores how to extract data consistently and reliably from SAP S/4HANA (with an ODP-enabled data source) to SAP Datasphere. 

Read the blog post

 

Community News

 

News and Other Resources for Community Members 

With Q&A forums, discussion areas, and blog boards, SAP Community provides valuable information for members looking to learn more about SAP. But what if you want to learn more about SAP Community itself? Make sure to subscribe to What's New, read our help section, watch our how-to videos, and take the Tour the SAP Community 2024 tutorial. 

 

Register for Exciting and Educational Events in June 

SAP Sapphire may be wrapping up in Orlando, but SAP Sapphire Barcelona is coming up soon -- plus our events section is filled with SAP CodeJam, SAP Inside Track, and SAP Stammtisch meetups, as well as other online and in-person events, including Hands-on with the btp CLI and APIs and ABAP CodeRetreat OnSite in Bern.

 

Dress for Success at SAP Community Events 

Speaking of SAP Community events (how's that for a segue?), have you signed up for an in-person session and you're not sure what to wear? Why not make a fashion statement with an item from our online store? There you'll find shirts, hoodies, and pins branded with SAP Community -- everything you need to ensure you're the sharpest-dressed attendee!

entry.title: Do you want to know how you can unlock the potential of AI?
entry.content:
🌀In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, everyone is keen on integrating Artificial Intelligence into their workflows; yet starting with a clear purpose is crucial. But: How to know where and how to start? ✨

The SAP AppHaus created two innovative workshop formats, the Business AI Explore and Business AI Discover/Design, crafted to guide people in identifying and defining impactful AI use cases tailored to their specific needs. The workshops emphasize that first of all, you need to understand the existing business challenges. Only then you can find out how AI might enhance your workflows and operations.

In this webinar, Karen Detken, creator of both the Business AI Discover/Design and the Business AI Explore workshop, will offer a comprehensive overview of both formats, provide practical examples, actionable tips, and share where to access the materials for conducting your own workshops.

📣Join us on June 18th to discover how you can effectively harness generative AI, and what you need consider beforehand.

Add to calendar: https://url.sap/8xtf5s

YouTube link: https://youtube.com/live/8ks3BXqH5XE?feature=share

entry.title: How to setup Business Application Studio for python development
entry.content:
I am writing this blog to describe the configuration procedures required for Python development in SAP Business Application Studio. This process is divided into the following four key steps:

  1. Creation of a Dev Space.
  2. Initial setup and configuration of the Dev Space
  3. Implementation of a Python application.
  4. Deployment of the application to BTP Cloud Foundry.

Pre-requisites

As considerable time has elapsed since the setup of my environment, I am unable to provide detailed pre-requisites.

  1. necessary services are active
  2. necessary authorizations are assigned

Procedures

1. Creation of Dev Space

On SAP BAS screen, click on "Create Dev Space" button.

With the below input, click on "Create Dev Space" button.

  1. Fill in the Dev Space name
  2. select "Basic" for application kind
  3. select Python Tools in Additional SAP Extensions

2. Initial setup and configuration of the Dev Space

Click on Dev Space name after the instance starts.

2.1. Create new project

Create Project. 

Though it is not MTA, just select "Basic Multitarget Application" and click on "Start".

Enter a project name and finish.

2.2. Create Python Environment

From command palette, select "Python: Create Environment".

Select Python interpreter path. I'm not sure which one is the best, but "/usr/bin/python3" was OK.

2.3. Install Jupyterlab

Though it is not mandatory step, I usually use jupyter for development.  Firstly, create terminal from command palette.

Then terminal runs and it automatically activate configured python.

Now show python version and installed packages.  
After that, upgrade pip and install jupyterlab.

 

 

(.venv) user: python-test $ python --version
Python 3.11.2
(.venv) user: python-test $ pip freeze
(.venv) user: python-test $ pip install --upgrade pip
Requirement already satisfied: pip in ./.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages (24.0)
(.venv) user: python-test $ pip install jupyterlab

 

 

Create a jupyter file and try jupyter.

3. Implementation of a Python application.

Implement simple python rest api app based on an article "Create simple Flask REST API using Cloud Foundry".

3.1. install Flask

Install Flask using pip.

 

 

(.venv) user: python-test $ pip install Flask
Collecting Flask
  Downloading flask-3.0.3-py3-none-any.whl.metadata (3.2 kB)
Collecting Werkzeug>=3.0.0 (from Flask)
  Downloading werkzeug-3.0.3-py3-none-any.whl.metadata (3.7 kB)
Requirement already satisfied: Jinja2>=3.1.2 in ./.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages (from Flask) (3.1.4)
Collecting itsdangerous>=2.1.2 (from Flask)
  Downloading itsdangerous-2.2.0-py3-none-any.whl.metadata (1.9 kB)
Collecting click>=8.1.3 (from Flask)
  Downloading click-8.1.7-py3-none-any.whl.metadata (3.0 kB)
Collecting blinker>=1.6.2 (from Flask)
  Downloading blinker-1.8.2-py3-none-any.whl.metadata (1.6 kB)
Requirement already satisfied: MarkupSafe>=2.0 in ./.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages (from Jinja2>=3.1.2->Flask) (2.1.5)
Downloading flask-3.0.3-py3-none-any.whl (101 kB)
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 101.7/101.7 kB 7.2 MB/s eta 0:00:00
Downloading blinker-1.8.2-py3-none-any.whl (9.5 kB)
Downloading click-8.1.7-py3-none-any.whl (97 kB)
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 97.9/97.9 kB 8.4 MB/s eta 0:00:00
Downloading itsdangerous-2.2.0-py3-none-any.whl (16 kB)
Downloading werkzeug-3.0.3-py3-none-any.whl (227 kB)
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 227.3/227.3 kB 13.8 MB/s eta 0:00:00
Installing collected packages: Werkzeug, itsdangerous, click, blinker, Flask
Successfully installed Flask-3.0.3 Werkzeug-3.0.3 blinker-1.8.2 click-8.1.7 itsdangerous-2.2.0

 

 

3.2. app.py

create "app.py".

 

 

from flask import Flask
import os

app = Flask(__name__)
# Port number is required to fetch from env variable
# http://docs.cloudfoundry.org/devguide/deploy-apps/environment-variable.html#PORT

cf_port = os.getenv("PORT")

# Only get method by default
@app.route('/')
def hello():
    return 'Hello World'

if __name__ == '__main__':
	if cf_port is None:
		app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=5000, debug=True)
	else:
		app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=int(cf_port), debug=True)

 

 

3.3. Test the app

In terminal, run "python app.py" and click on "Open in a New Tab" button.

"Hello World" is displayed in a new tab.

4. Deployment of the application to BTP Cloud Foundry

Create some files for deployment and deploy to BTP Cloud Foundry.

4.1. manifest.yml

manifest.yml for BTP Deployment.  Probably this is the minimum specs to run the app.

 

---
applications:
- memory: 128MB
  disk_quota: 512MB
  random-route: true

 

4.2 Procfile

Target python application.

 

web: python app.py

 

4.3. runtime.txt

Target python version.

 

python-3.11.X

 

4.4. requirements.txt

Necessary python packages.

 

Flask

 

4.5. .cfignore

".venv" is unnecessary to run cf app, so list up the directory.

 

.venv

 

4.6. Deploy

Deploy the app to cloud foundry.  My login target is eu10.

 

cf login -a https://api.cf.eu10.hana.ondemand.com
cf push <app name>

 

Deployed URL is shown on the terminal, so access the URL via browser.

If you don't need the app on cloud foundry, just delete the app.

cf delete <app name>

entry.title: Wow, SAP University Alliances´ offer is in the SAP Partners Benefit Catalogue!
entry.content:
SAP University Alliances organization offers SAP partners opportunities to connect with young talents and attract them to work for their organizations.

Engagement opportunities for SAP Partners

1. Guest Lecturer Assignment: get direct contact to talents via delivering a (guest) lecture for the group of students

  • Select university of your choice from the SAP University Alliances Member Universities and apply for guest lecture assignment

2. Internships: get access to talents by offering internships to students who showed extraordinary results

  • Please see also the Student Internships Sponsor Plan service on Partner Benefits Catalogue, which is exclusively for SAP Partners

3. Technical Teaching & Learning Infrastructure Sponsoring: increase your brand awareness through covering the services fees required to run live SAP systems for academic teaching

  • Select university of your choice from the SAP University Alliances Member Universities and become visible through being a challenge owner for a project seminar and/or provision of the access to BTP Free Tier system and/or sponsoring access to a different SAP solution.

4. Site visits: show their working environment and corporate culture by inviting lecturers and students to site visits.

  • Select university of your choice from the SAP University Alliances Member Universities and apply for site visit
  • Site visits could be accompanying other engagement opportunities and act as a ‘venue’ for course/project seminar final presentation

As this offer is specially for SAP Partners, if you are one and are interested, please, check out the application steps and details via the direct link in the SAP Partner Benefit Catalog, only visible for SAP Partners:  Connect with young talents through SAP University Alliances | Onboarding | Partner Benefits Catalog ...

 

In case of comments, pls, leave them here below or write to us at sapnextgen@sap.com  We look forward to having your feedback! 

Your SAP University Alliances team

entry.title: SAP’s IT organization is pulling back the curtain...
entry.content:
SAP’s IT organization is pulling back the curtain💃 to offer insights into SAP’s own enterprise. Learn best practices directly from the experts implementing our solutions. Did you know SAP itself is the first to test and use its own solutions? 

The internal IT organization at SAP serves as a living example of how SAP uses our own technology and empowers SAP's workforce with innovative solutions to bring out the best in SAP. The IT experts shine a light on what’s unique about SAP’s DNA that our customers trust to help transform businesses, entire industries, and the world. From Enterprise Architecture, AI, SAP Business Technology Platform, to Data, Analytics and more – join us to discover authentic insights about SAP.

See entire series: https://url.sap/bnuoax 

Don't miss the opportunity to peek behind the scenes and see how SAPs IT organisation works:

May 16: Explore how SAP's IT is Driving Innovation and Transformation
June 19: Our Cloud Business Journey’s Impact on Enterprise Architecture
July 4: How We Run SAP Business Technology Platform at Scale
July 11: Discover How SAP Accelerates Business Using SAP Build Solutions
Sept 11: Generative AI Platform Strategy - Enabling Innovation at scale
TBD: Business AI at SAP: How We Scale AI Adoption
TBD: Accelerating Insights to Action with AI at SAP
TBD: Data Products for AI Powered Decision Making
TBD: Powering HR Self-Services with Generative AI at SAP
TBD: Future-proofing SAP for Cloud Business Volumes

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Munich 2024 - Call for Papers 📢
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are delighted to announce that the SAP Inside Track Munich 2024 will take place on 12th of October. This is a fantastic opportunity for everyone to share their knowledge, exchange experiences, and connect with like-minded individuals. We invite you all to participate in the Call for Papers and share your expertise with the community. Whether you are an SAP expert, developer, consultant, or user, your contributions are welcome!

If you have any questions or need assistance with topic selection, feel free to email us at
contact@sitmuc.com. We are here to help!

Submission Details:

  • Submission Deadline: 13th of July 2024
  • Submission Process:
    Please submit your proposal using our online form:     https://sitmuc.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

There are various session types for your submissions:

  • Short Presentation (20 min)
  • Presentation (40 min)
  • Marketplace

In a Presentation, you will present a topic to the audience - demos, theory, live coding, or anything else that suits your expertise. In a Marketplace slot, you will share your topic in a “marketplace” breakout session where everyone is welcome to walk around, visit the various stands, and interact. You can choose the most suitable duration.

Please note that you are expected to attend the event in person in Munich.

Join us at SAP Inside Track Munich 2024 and actively contribute to the SAP community!
Looking forward to your submissions and to welcoming you in Munich.

Best regards,
sitMUC Orga team

entry.title: Brian Rice: A Clear Perspective - Finally Knowing Who I Truly Am
entry.content:
Smiling person on a teal background with the text "SAP Autism Inclusion Pledge - The Stories the Made Us"

At SAP Sapphire (2024) in Orlando, I had the honor to participate in a session, “From misunderstood to valued: The journey of neurodiversity in workplaces” to share my transformative journey and experiences to foster inclusion and unlock innovation.  

Going back a few years, in 2021, at the age of 41, I received a diagnosis that would reshape my understanding of myself—I was identified as being on the autism spectrum.  

I am blessed with a beautiful and loving wife, and two exceptional boys, both of whom are also on the spectrum.

Their experiences exemplify the diverse nature of autism, emphasizing that it is not a “one size fits all” condition. One son is non-verbal and sensitive to noise and brightness, stands in contrast to our other son, who started talking early and seeks sensory stimulation through physical contact. 

Throughout my life, I sensed that my brain processed information differently, leading to anxiety in social settings. However, the root cause remained elusive until I witnessed the challenges and strengths of my children.  

They provided a window into my own life—sensitivity to light, sounds, smells, and textures, coupled with the enjoyment yet draining nature of social interactions. I found solace in drilling into details, and often succumbed to hyper-focus on hobbies or new topics. My brain incessantly processed and sought information, hindering my ability to sleep unless mentally and physically exhausted. The struggle manifested as a late-night rumination on past and future conversations and events. 

Seeking answers, I enrolled in the University of Pennsylvania’s Adult Autism Spectrum (AAS) Program. The program, encompassing individual consultations and family interviews, aimed to decipher my diagnosis and outline a plan forward. The experience, marked by self-reflection and discovery, culminated in the revelation of my official diagnosis—a missing piece to my life's puzzle. 

Despite the clarity gained from the diagnosis, I hesitated to disclose it beyond my manager and immediate team.  

Gradually, I opened up, motivated by the overwhelming love and support from my family and colleagues at SAP. The pandemic, with its virtual meetings and ubiquitous video conferencing, heightened my discomfort due to my struggle with making eye contact. Disclosing my diagnosis became a crucial step, enabling collaboration with HR and my manager to create accommodations that allowed me to be authentic and comfortable in my work environment. 

The first half of my life saw academic success, marriage, family, and career advancement. As I embark on the second half, armed with newfound resources, I aspire to be an advocate for those on the autism spectrum. I aim to instill confidence in who I am, contribute to SAP's commitment to Diversity & Inclusion and serve as a role model for my children. 

In the realm of digital marketing, I leverage the analytical aspect of my brain to make data-driven decisions, reveling in the daily challenges that demand creative problem-solving. Managing a diverse team built on trust and respect has been profoundly rewarding, emphasizing the beauty of our differences and the generation of innovative ideas that challenge the norm. 

At SAP, we believe in bringing everything you are, and to become everything you want to be.  

Grateful for the support I've received, I recognize the importance of telling my story to raise awareness about the needs of others on the spectrum.  

As I navigate this journey, I aim to contribute to the ongoing work of creating an inclusive environment where everyone is treated with respect and has equal opportunities and access to resources.

This story is in Brian's own words. 

--- 

Useful Links 

  • SAP Autism Inclusion Pledge website  
  • Subscribe to occasional updates 
  • Diversity & Inclusion at SAP 
  • SAP Autism at Work career page

entry.title: The added value, the process & preparation of the Customer COE Team Accreditation
entry.content:
As SAP's product portfolio has evolved, so has the Customer COE concept in the 30 years since it was first introduced to our customers. Its goal has remained the same over time: To establish and develop an expert team on the customer side (the so-called “Customer Center of Expertise”) which bundles SAP know-how and competencies and brings them together across all business units.

As the Customer COE concept evolved, the Customer COE certification service became Customer COE Team Accreditation. Why this? Quite simply, performance is always the work of a team, combining strengths and networking with various stakeholders in the organization. The name of the service should reflect this, even if the intention, the content, and the process of the audit service remained the same.

Why has SAP been recommending establishing a Customer COE for three decades and develop the expert team in line with the SAP landscape?
In customer projects, we see time and time again that successful companies are realizing the full value of their SAP investment. Based on transparency, efficiency, clear responsibilities and agility, these organizations bring together the right resources and enable a continuous exchange. This is precisely where the added value of a Customer COE in the company lies. 

Having said this,what is the added value of the Customer COE team accreditation.
Can't see the wood for the trees" - this well-known saying is a good illustration of how difficult it can be to keep track of the many topics relating to your current and future SAP landscape. Particularly when setting up and developing your SAP expert team, the following questions need to be asked: Where is the best place to start? What topics should be covered as a minimum?

With the Customer COE Team Accreditation, we give you an orientation on which topics are important as a basis for implementing the stated goal of a Customer COE in practice. For established Customer COEs, further questions arise: Are the services offered by the Customer COE still aligned with my company's IT and SAP strategy? Is my Customer COE team well positioned organizationally in the future? Are there any changes in processes and responsibilities in the customer organization or at SAP that need to be considered? Which SAP solutions, services and tools have been launched or changed? And much more.

The preparation for the team accreditation already shows which topics are well established, which need to be addressed and which are still open. In short, the team accreditation will give you an indication of where there is a need for action. The questions in the audit guide and the requirements provided are helpful in this respect.

The result of the team accreditation can be used as a measure of maturity on which the Customer COE can build as it continues to develop. Because nothing is more constant than change!
Change is one of the current focus topics of the Customer COE framework from SAP. In particular, the consideration of where existing Customer COEs can place their focus to avoid being "left behind" and continue to be a valuable part of the customer organization.

This brings us to another aspect of a successfully passed team accreditation that should not be underestimated: internal marketing. Do good and talk about it. The certificate at the end of the successful audit is the icing on the cake – a confirmation from SAP that the expert team has successfully implemented the fundamentals. Bravo - keep up the good work!

Content of the Team Accreditation
The Customer COE Management Framework is the foundation of the Customer COE concept and therefore also of Team Accreditation. It clusters the various questions and topics for you, making them more tangible for later implementation. The associated insight documents summarize the most important topics for you and provide you with further sources.

Want something even more compact? Then the Customer COE Team Accreditation Guide is just right for you. Broken down into the individual areas of the Customer COE Management Framework pyramid, the guide provides you with reference points for the basic requirements for your SAP expert team - and does so in a sustainable manner. This means that the requirements relate to both the existing and your future SAP landscape. It is important to emphasize that the requirements are defined independently of the deployment model of your SAP solutions.

Preparation of the Customer COE Team accreditation and the audit process
Would you like to start with the team accreditation of your SAP expert team now? With pleasure. You will find all the information you need in the Customer COE portal page.

We will start with an overview of what you need to know about the Customer COE. Would you like to get your colleagues in the company on board? We will be happy to support you with the so-called ‘Initial Guidance Service’ - a free remote call with an SAP expert who will help your team and the departments involved with the CCOE concept.

The SAP Customer COE program team is also available to answer any questions you may have at any time. Contact us - COE.program@sap.com, we will be happy to help you.

Preparation is half the battle
The Customer COE Team Accreditation Guide will give you a first impression of the extent to which each topic area is established, in progress or open. Review the topics in the guide with your team. Include interface areas in the discussion. Create cross-departmental transparency about the processes and define responsibilities together.

Familiarize yourself with the SAP tools and services mentioned. The above-mentioned Customer COE Insight documents and the additional SAP information and best practices available in the Customer COE Portal pages will help you.

Answering of the audit questions.
The requirements addressed in the Team Accreditation Guide form the basis of the online questionnaire, which you can access via the Customer COE Portal.

To start the application, you need an S-user with the authorization ‘Report a Technical Issue’ (Create Case). In the online questionnaire, you specify how your Customer COE has implemented the requirements. The requirements of the Team Accreditation Guide can be found 1:1 in the online questionnaire. 

Good things take time: Allow the content to sink in and collaborate on the questionnaire across the organization. You can save your entries as a draft at any time, involve colleagues in the editing process or add attachments. As soon as you feel that you have made a comprehensive contribution, send the online questionnaire to SAP. This starts the actual audit process at SAP.

Customer COE auditing is an SAP remote service.
The remote service request runs through the general SAP internal dispatching process, which is documented via the automatically generated case. The case then provides you with all further information on the audit process, such as the name of the SAP auditor responsible for you.

The detailed review and assessment of your information is based on SAP best practices, which are summarized for you in the last column of the Team Accreditation Guide.

If necessary or if you have any queries about the information you have provided, the auditor will contact you by e-mail. They will contact the Customer COE Manager entered in the questionnaire. If you would like to involve other colleagues in the audit process, please pass on their contact details to the auditor for queries.

The role of the Customer COE Manager
The customer's Customer COE Manager has overall responsibility for the topics addressed to the SAP expert team. Even if there is always a team behind the performance and services of the Customer COE, the overall responsibility cannot be shared. The Customer COE Manager is maintained by the SAP Auditor in your customer master data at SAP and can be found in SAP for Me under ‘Users & Contacts’ -> My Important Contacts.

If you would like to have other colleagues maintained, such as the deputy, as a contact for your Customer COE at SAP, please also pass on the contact details to the auditor.

The audit results
The SAP auditor informs the customer's COE manager of the audit result by e-mail. This includes a benchmark with the results of all customers who have successfully completed the team accreditation. The e-mail will also include the Customer COE Team Accreditation certificate and more information on the exclusive offers for Customer COEs that SAP provides to honor the efforts made towards team accreditation.

Do you need further support? With pleasure!
You can find additional service offerings for setting up and developing your Customer COE in the Customer COE Portal Media Library, under the "Services" section. Your SAP contacts and the SAP Customer COE program team can help you find the right support for you.
